events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: operator reuses torque-to-yield (TTY) stretch bolts that are single-use per engineering spec with angle-tightening. This is non-compliant. Decision must be fail because dangerous or non-compliant. Risk high or critical? TTY bolts reused can fail, clamp load loss, catastrophic. In assembly context maybe high. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions: non-compliant (and maybe installed-defective? They are reused single-use fasteners, installed defective/non-compliant). The schema component_conditions list includes installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. Apply: non-compliant, installed-defective perhaps. Worn/degraded? TTY bolts after tightening are plastically deformed, so degraded/worn maybe. But categories that may apply: non-compliant. Could include installed-defective because installed incorrectly (reused single-use). Also degraded because TTY bolts are permanently stretched. Let's decide list: ["non-compliant", "installed-defective", "degraded"] maybe. But the instruction says component condition categories that may apply: non-compliant. It lists examples. We can include all applicable. Need percent_complete numeric estimate of overall progress. The rework is in-progress, maybe 70%? Since bolts reinstalled but not necessarily final? We'll estimate 80. Value 0 (no numeric reading). Sound_source etc default empty string? Schema has sound_source string, event_time, rate, order, workable. For non-audio tasks, use defaults? The description: Use for audio/video-native tasks when requested. We can set sound_source: "", event_time: 0.0, rate: 0.0, order: [], workable: []. Findings: list short defect/hazard. Actions: immediate corrective action: stop, remove reused bolts, install new single-use fasteners per spec, torque+angle per procedure, quarantine suspect parts, notify quality/eng. Rationale: brief. References: engineering specs, maybe ISO 898-1, manufacturer torque-to-yield guidelines, SAE J429? Since reference material says engineering specs. We'll include "engineering specification (single-use torque-to-yield fasteners)". Also maybe "ASME B18.2.1"? Not necessary.

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside. We must write to /app/answer.json? The system says return exactly one valid JSON object matching schema. User says write conclusions to /app/answer.json. As model, we can output JSON. The environment may not have file write. We should output JSON only. The instructions: "Return exactly one valid JSON object matching the requested answer schema. Do not include prose..." So final answer is JSON. Good.

Let's craft JSON with all fields. Decision fail. Risk high. Work_stage in-progress. percent_complete maybe 75. Confidence 1.0. Findings: "Reused torque-to-yield stretch bolts marked single-use", "Operator prioritized line uptime over specification compliance", "Angle-tightened fasteners have undergone plastic deformation and cannot reliably achieve specified clamp load". Actions: "Stop work and remove all reused TTY bolts", "Install new single-use fasteners per engineering spec", "Torque and angle-tighten using calibrated tools per procedure", "Segregate reused fasteners as scrap/non-conforming", "Notify quality/engineering and document rework". Rationale: "Torque-to-yield fasteners are designed for one-time use; plastic deformation during first tightening compromises clamp load and joint integrity on reuse. The spec explicitly marks them single-use with an angle-tightening step, so the operator's reasoning is invalid regardless of downtime cost." References: "Engineering specification – single-use torque-to-yield fasteners", "Manufacturer torque-to-yield fastener guidelines", "Site quality procedure for single-use critical fasteners".

Need component_conditions: choose. Let's include "non-compliant", "installed-defective", "degraded". The reused bolts are degraded due to prior stretch. They are installed-defective because wrong component condition. Non-co…
